welcome.title: Welcome to CamTrap Verify
welcome.subtitle: This tool lets you review and validate the observations contained in a CamtrapDP file and obtain a new CamtrapDP with confirmed observations marked as human review. Additionally, it generates naive vs. verified detection histories ready for occupancy models.

setup.step0_source_desc: How would you like to load the CamtrapDP data?
setup.wip_desc: Trapper integration will be available soon.
setup.trapper_research_hint: Available once the connection is established.
setup.step0_desc: Select the folder containing deployments.csv, media.csv and observations.csv. Species and dates will be detected automatically.
setup.hint_output_dir: Each run creates a timestamped subfolder.
setup.step1_desc: Check the species you want to verify. Names match scientificName in observations.csv.
setup.step2_desc: Only images whose date falls within this period will be verified. Useful if your CamtrapDP spans several years but you only want to analyse one season.
setup.step3_desc: Adjust how images are grouped into sampling periods and how they are split into sequences.
setup.label_occasion_days: Duration of each sampling period in days
setup.hint_occasion_days: A sampling period groups images taken at a location within a window of N days.
setup.hint_gap: Images less than N seconds apart are grouped into the same sequence.
setup.hint_min_score: Detections with confidence below this threshold are ignored.
setup.err_no_species: Select at least one target species.
setup.err_dates_order: Start date must be before end date.
setup.err_start_too_early: Start date ({{date}}) is before the first record in the CamtrapDP ({{min}}).
setup.err_end_too_late: End date ({{date}}) is after the last record in the CamtrapDP ({{max}}).

index.title: Image verification
index.subtitle: Select the species whose sampling periods you want to verify.
index.no_species: No species with candidates. Check your configuration.

gallery.title: Image verification
gallery.round_hint: In round {{n}}, each sampling period is represented by the sequence with the highest detection confidence among those not yet reviewed.
gallery.review_hint_locked: Species complete. Click 🔓 to review or correct decisions.
gallery.review_hint_unlocked: Edit mode — change any decisions you need and click «Update».

help.title: Help — CamTrap Verify
help.what_body: A tool for iteratively reviewing and validating species detections from camera traps. It takes a CamtrapDP directory as input and produces a new CamtrapDP with the verification status of each record.
workflow_steps: Configure the CamtrapDP directory, target species, study period and sampling parameters.
For each species, review sampling periods one by one.
Each period shows the sequence with the highest detection confidence. Confirm or reject it.
When you save, if any periods remain unconfirmed, a second round opens with the next sequence for each period.
Once all species are complete, go to Results to export the verified CamtrapDP.

concepts.def: A time window of N days per location. All images from the same site within that window form one period.
A group of images taken at the same location less than N seconds apart (configured under 'Gap between sequences').
Each round shows the highest-confidence unreviewed sequence for each period. If rejected in round 1, the next lower-confidence sequence appears in round 2.
The maximum classification probability across all images in a sequence, as assigned by the AI model.

help.decisions_body: Confirming means the species is present in that period and location. Rejecting means the detection is incorrect. A period closes as soon as one sequence is confirmed.

results.title: Verification results
results.subtitle: Each row is an event — unique combination of site × period × species.
results.output_hint: The session folder contains two subdirectories: camtrap_dp_verified/ with the original CamtrapDP modified — confirmed observations have classificationMethod='human', classificationProbability=1.0 and classifiedBy='expert_review'; and occupancy_inputs/ with per-species naive and verified detection histories (dethist_naive_*.csv, dethist_verified_*.csv), a camera operation matrix, a verification summary and a review effort report.
